data.content:
### Preview Environment Deployments

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/local-env

Preview environments allow you to deploy and test changes in a live setting without affecting production. Vercel automatically creates preview deployments when pushing to non-production branches or creating pull requests.

```APIDOC
## Preview Environment Deployments

### Description
Preview environments provide live testing of changes without affecting production. Automatically triggered by branch pushes or pull requests.

### Automatic Trigger Conditions
- Push to a branch other than production branch (commonly `main`)
- Create a pull request on GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket
- Deploy via CLI without the `-prod` flag

### CLI Command
```bash
vercel
```

### Generated URLs
Each deployment receives an automatically generated URL:
- **Branch-specific URL** - Points to latest changes on that branch
- **Commit-specific URL** - Points to exact deployment of that commit

### URL Display
Links appear in:
- Git provider PR comments
- Vercel Dashboard
```

--------------------------------

### Project Deployment Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Configure deployment settings for your Vercel project, including deployment type and preview URL behavior. This endpoint allows you to specify which deployments should generate preview URLs and how they are distributed.

```APIDOC
## Project Deployment Configuration

### Description
Configure deployment type and preview URL settings for your Vercel project.

### Request Body
#### deploymentType
- **deploymentType** (string) - Required - The deployment type configuration
  - Enum values:
    - `preview` - Preview deployments only
    - `production` - Production deployments only
    - `all` - All deployment types
    - `prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews` - Production URLs and all preview deployments
    - `all_except_custom_domains` - All deployments except custom domains

### Request Example
```json
{
  "deploymentType": "all"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **deploymentType** (string) - The configured deployment type
```

--------------------------------

### Staging Prefix Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/user/get-the-user

Configure and retrieve the staging prefix used in preview deployment URLs. This setting applies to all Preview deployments created by the user account.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/user/staging-prefix

### Description
Retrieve the staging prefix configuration for preview deployments. This prefix is used in the URL of Preview deployments created by the user account.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/user/staging-prefix

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **stagingPrefix** (string) - Prefix used in preview deployment URLs

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "stagingPrefix": "preview"
}
```

---

## PUT /api/user/staging-prefix

### Description
Update the staging prefix for preview deployments. The new prefix will be applied to all subsequent Preview deployments.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
/api/user/staging-prefix

### Request Body
- **stagingPrefix** (string) - Required - New staging prefix value

### Request Example
```json
{
  "stagingPrefix": "staging"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **stagingPrefix** (string) - Updated staging prefix value

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "stagingPrefix": "staging"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Pull preview environment variables with vercel pull

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Downloads the latest preview environment variables and project settings from Vercel cloud to the local cache. Useful for testing preview deployments locally.

```bash
vercel pull --environment=preview
```

--------------------------------

### Project Deployment Features Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-an-existing-project

Configure deployment-related features including preview feedback, production feedback, and affected projects deployments optimization. These settings control user experience and deployment behavior.

```APIDOC
## Project Deployment Features Configuration

### Description
Manage deployment feature flags and options for project-level feedback toolbars and deployment optimization.

### Configuration Properties

#### sourceFilesOutsideRootDirectory
- **Type**: Boolean
- **Description**: Indicates if there are source files outside of the root directory.

#### enablePreviewFeedback
- **Type**: Boolean (Nullable)
- **Description**: Opt-in to preview toolbar on the project level.

#### enableProductionFeedback
- **Type**: Boolean (Nullable)
- **Description**: Opt-in to production toolbar on the project level.

#### enableAffectedProjectsDeployments
- **Type**: Boolean
- **Description**: Opt-in to skip deployments when there are no changes to the root directory and its dependencies.

#### skipGitConnectDuringLink
- **Type**: Boolean
- **Deprecated**: true
- **Description**: Opts-out of the message prompting a CLI user to connect a Git repository in `vercel link`.

### Configuration Example
```json
{
  "sourceFilesOutsideRootDirectory": false,
  "enablePreviewFeedback": true,
  "enableProductionFeedback": true,
  "enableAffectedProjectsDeployments": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Pull Preview Environment Variables to local .env.local

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

This command downloads environment variables specifically configured for the `preview` environment from the cloud. These variables are then written to the default local `.env.local` file, aiding in testing preview deployments locally.

```terminal
vercel env pull --environment=preview
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Server-Side Fetch Requests for Protected Vercel Deployments (JavaScript)

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection

This JavaScript example illustrates how to perform server-side fetch requests when a Vercel deployment is protected. It shows constructing a fetch call using the incoming request's origin and manually passing request cookies to maintain user authentication, essential for accessing protected resources.

```javascript
const headers = { cookie: <incoming request header cookies> };
fetch('<incoming request origin>/some/path', { headers });
```

--------------------------------

### Preview Deployment Suffix Event Payload

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/user/list-user-events

Event payload for preview deployment suffix configuration changes. Tracks updates to the custom suffix used in preview deployment URLs.

```APIDOC
## Event Payload - Preview Deployment Suffix Change

### Description
Represents a change in the preview deployment URL suffix configuration.

### Properties
- **previewDeploymentSuffix** (string | null) - Optional - Current preview deployment suffix
- **previousPreviewDeploymentSuffix** (string | null) - Optional - Previous preview deployment suffix

### Response Example
{
  "previewDeploymentSuffix": "pr",
  "previousPreviewDeploymentSuffix": "preview"
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /v6/deployments

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Retrieves a list of deployments with support for extensive filtering options including project ID, environment target, deployment state, and timestamp ranges. This endpoint requires bearer token authentication and returns paginated results.

```APIDOC
## GET /v6/deployments

### Description
Retrieves a list of deployments from Vercel with comprehensive filtering capabilities. Supports filtering by project, environment, state, timestamp ranges, branch, and more.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`https://api.vercel.com/v6/deployments`

### Authentication
- **Type**: Bearer Token (HTTP)
- **Location**: Authorization header
- **Description**: Default authentication mechanism required for all requests

### Parameters

#### Query Parameters
- **app** (string) - Optional - Name of the deployment. Example: `docs`
- **projectId** (string) - Optional - Filter deployments from the given ID or name. Example: `QmXGTs7mvAMMC7WW5ebrM33qKG32QK3h4vmQMjmY`
- **projectIds** (array of strings) - Optional - Filter deployments from the given project IDs (max 20, min 1). Cannot be used when projectId is specified. Example: `["prj_123", "prj_456"]`
- **target** (string) - Optional - Filter deployments based on the environment. Example: `production`
- **state** (string) - Optional - Filter deployments based on their state. Allowed values: `BUILDING`, `ERROR`, `INITIALIZING`, `QUEUED`, `READY`, `CANCELED`. Example: `BUILDING,READY`
- **limit** (number) - Optional - Maximum number of deployments to list from a request. Example: `10`
- **since** (number) - Optional - Get deployments created after this JavaScript timestamp. Example: `1540095775941`
- **until** (number) - Optional - Get deployments created before this JavaScript timestamp. Example: `1540095775951`
- **from** (number) - Optional - DEPRECATED. Gets the deployment created after this Date timestamp. Example: `1612948664566`
- **to** (number) - Optional - DEPRECATED. Gets the deployment created before this Date timestamp. Example: `1612948664566`
- **users** (string) - Optional - Filter deployments based on users who have created the deployment. Example: `kr1PsOIzqEL5Xg6M4VZcZosf,K4amb7K9dAt5R2vBJWF32bmY`
- **rollbackCandidate** (boolean) - Optional - Filter deployments based on their rollback candidacy
- **branch** (string) - Optional - Filter deployments based on the branch name
- **sha** (string) - Optional - Filter deployments based on the SHA
- **teamId** (string) - Optional - The Team identifier to perform the request on behalf of. Example: `team_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l`
- **slug** (string) - Optional - The Team slug to perform the request on behalf of. Example: `my-team-url-slug`

### Request Example
```
GET /v6/deployments?projectId=QmXGTs7mvAMMC7WW5ebrM33qKG32QK3h4vmQMjmY&target=production&limit=10&state=READY
Host: api.vercel.com
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN_HERE
```

### Response

#### Success Response (200 OK)
Returns an array of deployment objects with the following structure:
- **deployments** (array) - List of deployment objects
  - **id** (string) - Unique deployment identifier
  - **name** (string) - Deployment name
  - **project** (string) - Project name
  - **state** (string) - Current deployment state
  - **target** (string) - Target environment
  - **url** (string) - Deployment URL
  - **createdAt** (number) - Creation timestamp
  - **creator** (object) - Creator information

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "deployments": [
    {
      "id": "dpl_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l",
      "name": "my-project",
      "project": "my-project",
      "state": "READY",
      "target": "production",
      "url": "my-project.vercel.app",
      "createdAt": 1540095775941,
      "creator": {
        "uid": "user_123",
        "username": "example-user"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Error Responses

#### 401 Unauthorized
- Invalid or missing bearer token

#### 400 Bad Request
- Invalid query parameters
- conflicting parameters (e.g., both projectId and projectIds specified)

#### 404 Not Found
- Project or team not found
```

--------------------------------

### Get Deployments with Vercel SDK - TypeScript

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Retrieve deployments using TypeScript SDK with bearer token authentication and multiple filtering options. Supports pagination, project IDs array, team ID, and slug parameters. Returns deployment results with metadata including UIDs, URLs, and timestamps.

```typescript
import { Vercel } from "@vercel/sdk";

const vercel = new Vercel({
  bearerToken: "<YOUR_BEARER_TOKEN_HERE>",
});

async function run() {
  const result = await vercel.deployments.getDeployments({
    app: "docs",
    from: 1612948664566,
    limit: 10,
    projectId: "QmXGTs7mvAMMC7WW5ebrM33qKG32QK3h4vmQMjmY",
    projectIds: [
      "prj_123",
      "prj_456",
    ],
    target: "production",
    to: 1612948664566,
    users: "kr1PsOIzqEL5Xg6M4VZcZosf,K4amb7K9dAt5R2vBJWF32bmY",
    since: 1540095775941,
    until: 1540095775951,
    state: "BUILDING,READY",
    teamId: "team_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l",
    slug: "my-team-url-slug",
  });

  console.log(result);
}

run();
```

--------------------------------

### GET /v3/deployments/{idOrUrl}/events

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Retrieves build and deployment events for a specific deployment. Supports filtering by timestamp ranges, status codes, and build IDs. Can stream live events in real-time when the follow parameter is enabled.

```APIDOC
## GET /v3/deployments/{idOrUrl}/events

### Description
Retrieve events for a deployment. This endpoint returns build logs and deployment events, with support for real-time streaming, filtering by time ranges, status codes, and build information.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
https://api.vercel.com/v3/deployments/{idOrUrl}/events

### Authentication
- **Bearer Token** (Required) - HTTP Bearer authentication using Authorization header

### Parameters

#### Path Parameters
- **idOrUrl** (string) - Required - The unique identifier or hostname of the deployment. Example: `dpl_5WJWYSyB7BpgTj3EuwF37WMRBXBtPQ2iTMJHJBJyRfd`

#### Query Parameters
- **direction** (enum: backward | forward) - Optional - Order of the returned events based on timestamp. Default: `forward`. Example: `backward`
- **follow** (enum: 0 | 1) - Optional - When enabled (1), returns live events as they happen. Example: `1`
- **limit** (number) - Optional - Maximum number of events to return. Use `-1` to return all available logs. Example: `100`
- **name** (string) - Optional - Deployment build ID to filter events. Example: `bld_cotnkcr76`
- **since** (number) - Optional - Timestamp for when build logs should be pulled from. Example: `1540095775941`
- **until** (number) - Optional - Timestamp for when build logs should be pulled up until. Example: `1540106318643`
- **statusCode** (string | number) - Optional - HTTP status code range to filter events by. Example: `5xx`
- **delimiter** (enum: 0 | 1) - Optional - Event delimiter flag. Example: `1`
- **builds** (enum: 0 | 1) - Optional - Include build events. Example: `1`
- **teamId** (string) - Optional - The Team identifier to perform the request on behalf of. Example: `team_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l`
- **slug** (string) - Optional - The Team slug to perform the request on behalf of. Example: `my-team-url-slug`

### Request Example
```bash
curl -X GET 'https://api.vercel.com/v3/deployments/dpl_5WJWYSyB7BpgTj3EuwF37WMRBXBtPQ2iTMJHJBJyRfd/events?direction=backward&follow=1&limit=100&name=bld_cotnkcr76&since=1540095775941&until=1540106318643&statusCode=5xx' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_BEARER_TOKEN'
```

### Response

#### Success Response (200)
Returns a stream of deployment and build events with the following structure:
- **timestamp** (number) - Unix timestamp of the event
- **type** (string) - Event type (build, deployment, etc.)
- **message** (string) - Event message or log output
- **buildId** (string) - Associated build identifier
- **statusCode** (number) - HTTP status code if applicable

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "events": [
    {
      "timestamp": 1540095775941,
      "type": "build.start",
      "message": "Build started",
      "buildId": "bld_cotnkcr76"
    },
    {
      "timestamp": 1540106318643,
      "type": "build.complete",
      "message": "Build completed successfully",
      "buildId": "bld_cotnkcr76",
      "statusCode": 200
    }
  ]
}
```

### Code Examples

#### Go
```go
package main

import(
	"os"
	"github.com/vercel/vercel"
	"context"
	"github.com/vercel/vercel/models/operations"
	"log"
)

func main() {
    s := vercel.New(
        vercel.WithSecurity(os.Getenv("VERCEL_BEARER_TOKEN")),
    )

    ctx := context.Background()
    res, err := s.Deployments.GetDeploymentEvents(ctx, operations.GetDeploymentEventsRequest{
        IDOrURL: "dpl_5WJWYSyB7BpgTj3EuwF37WMRBXBtPQ2iTMJHJBJyRfd",
        Direction: operations.DirectionBackward.ToPointer(),
        Follow: vercel.Float64(1),
        Limit: vercel.Float64(100),
        Name: vercel.String("bld_cotnkcr76"),
        Since: vercel.Float64(1540095775941),
        Until: vercel.Float64(1540106318643),
        StatusCode: vercel.Pointer(operations.CreateStatusCodeStr(
            "5xx",
        )),
        Delimiter: vercel.Float64(1),
        Builds: vercel.Float64(1),
    })
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    if res != nil {
        // handle response
    }
}
```

#### TypeScript
```typescript
import { Vercel } from "@vercel/sdk";

const vercel = new Vercel({
  bearerToken: "<YOUR_BEARER_TOKEN_HERE>",
});

async function run() {
  const result = await vercel.deployments.getDeploymentEvents({
    idOrUrl: "dpl_5WJWYSyB7BpgTj3EuwF37WMRBXBtPQ2iTMJHJBJyRfd",
    direction: "backward",
    follow: 1,
    limit: 100,
    name: "bld_cotnkcr76",
    since: 1540095775941,
    until: 1540106318643,
    statusCode: "5xx",
    delimiter: 1,
    builds: 1,
  });

  console.log(result);
}

run();
```

### Error Handling
- **400 Bad Request** - Invalid parameters or malformed request
- **401 Unauthorized** - Missing or invalid bearer token
- **404 Not Found** - Deployment not found
- **500 Internal Server Error** - Server-side error
```

--------------------------------

### Disable GitHub Auto-Alias for Preview Deployments

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration/git-configuration

Set github.autoAlias to false to disable automatic aliasing and create preview deployments upon merge instead. This setting is deprecated in favor of the dashboard workflow for staging and promoting production deployments.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "github": {
    "autoAlias": false
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Vercel Deployment Task Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/git/vercel-for-azure-pipelines

Configure the Vercel deployment task with project credentials, authentication tokens, and deployment options. This task handles deployment to Vercel with support for both production and preview deployments.

```APIDOC
## Vercel Deployment Task Configuration

### Description
Configures and executes Vercel deployment operations with authentication and deployment preferences.

### Input Properties

#### Configuration Parameters
- **vercelProjectId** (string) - Optional - The ID of your Vercel Project. Can alternatively be set as environment variable VERCEL_PROJECT_ID
- **vercelOrgId** (string) - Optional - The ID of your Vercel Org. Can alternatively be set as environment variable VERCEL_ORG_ID
- **vercelToken** (string) - Optional - A Vercel personal access token with deploy permissions for your Vercel Project. Can alternatively be set as environment variable VERCEL_TOKEN
- **vercelCWD** (string) - Optional - The working directory where the Vercel deployment task will run. Defaults to System.DefaultWorkingDirectory. Can alternatively be set as environment variable VERCEL_CWD

#### Deployment Options
- **production** (boolean) - Optional - Boolean value specifying if the task should create a production deployment. When omitted or set to false, creates preview deployments
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Boolean value that enables the --debug option for internal Vercel CLI operations

### Output Variables

#### Response Data
- **deploymentTaskMessage** (string) - The message output taken from the deployment. Can be used in tasks that follow
```
